mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, increasing present values and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect operates continuously without requiring a threshold trigger.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS declines over 3 months (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the signal is below 0.03 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.0%, the 5-day forward return is not negative with statistical significance (t-test p > 0.10), or the signal corrects within 3 months without a negative impact.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust consumer and residential demand, which drives economic growth and boosts corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The joint behaviour is required for the signal.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST increase MoM, the 60-day forward return is not positive in at least 60% of instances, or the correlation between the joint signal and forward returns is not significant (Spearman rank correlation < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth (INDPRO YoY) exceeds 2%, it signals strong manufacturing activity and economic momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional, active only during manufacturing expansion periods.
failure_condition: In periods where INDPRO YoY > 2%, the 20-day forward return is not positive with a win rate above 55%, or the signal reverses direction in the subsequent quarter.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate above 1.5% that increases over 3 months signals monetary tightening, which raises the cost of capital and depresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The threshold trigger is the rate level and its upward trend.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the negative return rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects a strengthening labor market, boosting aggregate demand and corporate profits, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This trend signal operates continuously.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following positive PAYEMS changes is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05), or the Rank IC of the signal is negative over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1 YoY) exceeds 3% and retail sales growth (RSAFS YoY) remains above 5%, the economy is in a strong late-cycle expansion. This combination signals sustained demand that pushes equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days. The regime-conditional interaction filters out weaker growth periods.
regime_hint: strong late-cycle expansion (GDPC1 YoY > 3% and RSAFS YoY > 5%)
failure_condition: In the regime defined, the 20-day forward return is not positive in at least 60% of observations, or the signal has a negative Sharpe ratio over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining retail sales (RSAFS) and rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a contractionary demand shock: higher borrowing costs suppress consumption while falling sales reduce earnings, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This interaction effect is not decomposable to single-series signals.
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines and FEDFUNDS rises simultaneously, the 20-day forward return is negative in less than 55% of cases, or the joint signal's predictive power is not materially better than either series alone (p-value of interaction term > 0.10 in regression).
